heading: Medal of honor: presentation of Medal of Honor Flag
label: 7285 Medal of honor: presentation of Medal of Honor Flag
text: The President shall provide for the presentation of the Medal of Honor Flag designated under to each person to whom a medal of honor is awarded under . Presentation of the flag shall be made at the same time as the presentation of the medal under section 7271 or 7282(a) of this title. In the case of a posthumous presentation of the medal, the flag shall be presented to the person to whom the medal is presented.section 903 of title 36section 7271 of this title
notes: Amendments2018—, substituted “section 7271” for “section 3741” and “section 7271 or 7282(a)” for “section 3741 or 3752(a)”.Pub. L. 115–232, § 809(a), renumbered as this section.Pub. Presentation of Flag for Prior Recipients of Medal of Honor, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–364, div. A, title V, § 555(b)120 Stat. 2217Oct. 17, 2006“(1).—Living recipientsThe President shall provide for the presentation of the Medal of Honor Flag as expeditiously as possible after the date of the enactment of this Act [] to each living recipient of the Medal of Honor who has not already received a Medal of Honor Flag.Oct. 17, 2006“(2).—Survivors of deceased recipientsIn the case of presentation of the Medal of Honor Flag for a recipient of the Medal of Honor who was awarded the Medal of Honor before the date of the enactment of this Act [] and who is deceased as of such date (or who dies after such date and before the presentation required by paragraph (1)), the President shall provide for posthumous presentation of the Medal of Honor Flag, upon written application therefor, to the primary living next of kin, as determined under regulations or procedures prescribed by the Secretary of Defense for the purposes of this paragraph (and notwithstanding the amendments made by paragraph (2) of subsection (a) [amending this section]).Oct. 17, 2006“(3).—Medal of honor flagIn this subsection, the term ‘Medal of Honor Flag’ means the flag designated under , United States Code.”section 903 of title 36
